reviews.review_msg: If you seek good classroom discussion or deep textual analysis in an upper level PHIL class, avoid at all costs. In discussion, Dr. McPherran is content to give short answers that stop any subsequent questioning, rather than follow along with students' lines of thought; as a consequence, neither deep thinking nor good discussion is achieved.

Very helpful and knowledgeable. Lectures can go a little slow some days depending on the topic and the people in your class. Basically do the readings and ask questions. If there is something that you don`t understand he will help you.
An awesome prof, definitely makes the class worthwhile !

Great teacher! Very approachable, knowledgeable and inspiring. His classes are very helpful, fun and he is also a fair marker. His goal is to make students to understand ancient Greek philosophy and why western civilization is how it is today. He is trying to teach students how to read philosophy, think rational and stand on their own feet.
....smart, helpful, inspiring, knowledgable, and a little hot (for an old guy).
I'm taking his class phil-150 now, his class is awesome! He's the best teacher i've ever met at SFU!

Very inspired professor; genuinely likes teaching and shows it.  Has a lot of knowledge of Greek history and the milieu in which western philosophy was birthed. Make sure you understand standard form arguments: you will be required to put arguments into standard form.  Overall a great guy, fair marker, knowledgeable and clear professor.
great prof! on the last day of the semester everyone stood up clapping for him! has a lot of interest towards his class and a great sense of humor.
Oh, you know... Personable, fair, a good sense of humour, and keeps the material interesting.
